The Same Natural Targets.In the First One the Beam
Cross Section was Defined by a Final Collimator with
A 0.4-Cm Diameter and the Target-Chamber Distance was
60 Cm For Al,Ni and Fe and 100 Cm For Carbon.In the
Second One We Increased the Resolution (NEW DISTANCES
WAS 90 AND 120-CM RESPECTIVELY) And Used a Final
Collimator with a 0.5-Cm Diameter.In this Work We
Consider in Detail Only the Second Series of
A 41.9-MeV Beam from the Cyclotron in
Milan was Used. The Polarized Beam was Obtained by
Scattering at About 57.5 Degree in the Horizontal Plane
On Carbon Target and by Means of an Analyzing Magnet
